def: Total time spent on digital devices (phone, laptop, tablet, TV) per day.
The process of gaining proficiency in a new ability through practice and learning.
Engaging with content purposefully (taking notes, practicing, applying), as opposed to passive consumption (scrolling, watching without engagement).
Software powered by artificial intelligence that assists with tasks like writing, coding, analysis, and creation.
Education where the learner chooses what, when, and how to learn, often outside formal institutions.
Bite-sized learning units, typically 5-15 minutes, optimized for attention and retention.
The habit of continuously scrolling through negative or trivial content, often unconsciously.
The skill of crafting effective inputs for AI systems to get desired outputs.
Maintaining a healthy relationship with technology, balancing screen time with physical and mental health.
A structured plan of learning resources and milestones to develop a specific skill over time.
